cards.title: When Your Adult Child Pulls Away
cards.body: A parent wonders why their adult son resists spending time together, despite weekly contact. An expert suggests examining whether unspoken pressure or unresolved feelings about parenthood might be at play.

cards.title: Managing Worry on Family Holidays
cards.body: A parent learns to manage anxiety and catastrophic thinking while on vacation with children. When a real emergency occurs, she discovers she has the resilience to handle it.

cards.title: How Childhood Trauma Shapes Adult Relationships
cards.body: A man with narcissistic and avoidant parents struggles with healthy relationship patterns into adulthood. His own breakup forces him to confront the dysfunctional patterns learned in childhood.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird—Or Selfish
cards.body: Prejudices against only children and their parents persist despite growing numbers. A parent defends the choice to have one child and challenges misconceptions about social development.

cards.title: Passport Issues for British Children Abroad
cards.body: A British parent living in Switzerland faces unexpected rejection of her youngest child's passport application despite identical documentation for her other two children. She navigates bureaucratic challenges in securing travel documents.

cards.title: Navigating Blended Family Dynamics
cards.body: Adult children face the challenge of accepting a parent's new partner after separation. The advice explores balancing fairness with what's best for family relationships.

cards.title: You Can Talk to Your Child About Anything
cards.body: Parents often avoid difficult conversations with children, but experts say these talks don't need to be avoided. Open dialogue builds trust and helps children navigate complex emotions.

cards.title: Music Performance Anxiety in Children
cards.body: Stage fright in young performers often stems from an internalized critical parent voice. Breaking this cycle requires honest self-analysis and healing from parental perfectionism.

cards.title: Pathological Demand Avoidance in Children
cards.body: Families can reshape and improve a child's PDA-linked patterns with the right support and treatment. These behavioral cycles aren't permanent with proper intervention.

cards.title: Childhood Chaos and Adult Safety
cards.body: Adults who grew up in chronically unstable homes may seek chaos to feel in control and calm. Understanding this pattern helps break cycles of dysfunction in parenting.

cards.title: Family Narratives Help Children Understand
cards.body: Families create stories to help make sense of experiences children aren't yet equipped to understand. These narratives are essential for processing trauma and building identity.

cards.title: Trauma-Driven Relationship Cycles in Couples
cards.body: Some couples become trapped in interlocking traumatic patterns neither fully recognizes. Identifying these cycles can reduce conflict and strengthen the family unit.
